Burst credit summary

Burst credit notice

Burst credit notices matter when they explain which workloads can spend temporary credit pools above baseline throughput, how credits are replenished, and what operators should change in pacing policy before the new burst-credit window opens.

Useful notices also document burn-rate dashboards, retry smoothing, and the fallback routing controls teams should keep enabled while burst-credit behavior is monitored.
